zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord Richard VI Vernon van Haddon於1362年出生在英國德比夏爾哈登 霍爾時，他的父親Richard de Vernon I22歲，母親Juliana de Pembridge13歲。他於1388年與Joanna verch Rhys Heiress of STACKPOLE在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1403年7月23日在英國史洛普爾史福斯波利享年41歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord Richard VI Vernon van Haddon于1362年出生在英国德比夏爾哈登 霍爾时，他的父亲Richard de Vernon I22岁，母亲Juliana de Pembridge13岁。他于1388年与Joanna verch Rhys Heiress of STACKPOLE在英国英格兰斯塔福爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1403年7月23日在英国史洛普爾史福斯波利享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: Vernon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Vernon in Eure, France, named from the Gaulish element ver(n) ‘alder’ + the Gallo-Roman locative suffix -o (genitive -ōnis).
French: habitational name from the same place as in 1 above or from any of several other places in France with the same name and etymology.

variants.name: Varnon
meanings: English (Leicestershire and Northamptonshire): variant of   Vernon  .

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Haddon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places, in Derbyshire, Northamptonshire, Huntingdonshire, and Devon, named with Old English hǣth ‘heathland, heather’ + dūn ‘hill’, or from Haddon in Cambridgeshire, which is probably named from the Old English personal name Headda + dūn.
Scottish: variant of   Hadden  .

variants.name: Hadden
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the barony of Hadden, Halden, or Haulden (Roxburghshire), or a variant of   Haldane  .
English: variant of   Haddon  .
Irish (Armagh and Tyrone): though mainly Scottish, this surname is sometimes used as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÉidín ‘descendant of Éidín’ (see Hayden ).

variants.name: Haden
meanings: English (West Midlands): variant of   Haddon   and   Haydon  .
Irish: variant of   Hayden  .
Perhaps an altered form of German   Hadden   or   Heiden  .

variants.name: Hedden
meanings: English: habitational name from Hedon (East Yorkshire), Headon (Nottinghamshire), Black Heddon (Northumberland), Heddon on the Wall (Northumberland), Heddon in Filleigh (Devon), or Headon Hill (Isle of Wight). Most of the placenames derive from Old English hǣth ‘heath, heather’ + dūn ‘hill’, though the Nottinghamshire placename probably has hēah ‘high’ as the initial element.
English: variant of   Haddon  .
Scottish and Irish: variant of   Hadden  .

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Lorraine   or variant spelling of   Laurie .

variants.name: Lorraine
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin denoting a migrant from the province of Lorraine in eastern France. This derives its name from Latin Lotharingia ‘territory of the people of Lothar’. The latter is a Germanic personal name derived from hlud ‘fame’ + heri, hari ‘army’. Lorraine began to be used as a girl's name in Scotland in the 19th century, and for a time in the second half of the 20th century enjoyed great popularity, which has since waned.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: VI
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord Richard VI Vernon van Haddon was born in 1362, in Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, England, his father, Richard de Vernon I, was 22 and his mother, Juliana de Pembridge, was 13. He married Joanna verch Rhys Heiress of STACKPOLE in 1388, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 23 July 1403, in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord Richard VI Vernon van Haddon est né en 1362, à Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Vernon I, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Juliana de Pembridge, avait 13 ans. Il a épousé Joanna verch Rhys Heiress of STACKPOLE en 1388, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 23 juillet 1403, à Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord Richard VI Vernon van Haddon 1362 in Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de Vernon I 22 und seine Mutter Juliana de Pembridge 13. Er hat 1388 in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Joanna verch Rhys Heiress of STACKPOLE geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 23. Juli 1403 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Richard VI Vernon van Haddon è nato il nel 1362 nel Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, England, il suo padre, Richard de Vernon I, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Juliana de Pembridge, aveva 13 anni. Lui sposato Joanna verch Rhys Heiress of STACKPOLE nel 1388, nel Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 23 luglio 1403, nel Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, all'età di 41 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord Richard VI Vernon van Haddonが1362年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアハッドオン ハルで生まれた時，父Richard de Vernon Iは22歳，母Juliana de Pembridgeは13歳であった。1388年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアでJoanna verch Rhys Heiress of STACKPOLEと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1403年7月23日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアシュリュースブリーで41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Richard VI Vernon van Haddon(이)가 1362년 영국 더비실르 하던 홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Vernon I(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Juliana de Pembridge(은)는 13세였다. 그는 1388년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 Joanna verch Rhys Heiress of STACKPOLE(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1403년 7월 23일 영국 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Joanna verch Rhys Heiress of STACKPOLE em 1388, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 23 de julho de 1403, em Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 41 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord Richard VI Vernon van Haddon родился в 1362 г. в местности хаддон холл, дирбишир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard de Vernon I было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Juliana de Pembridge, 13. Он женился на женщине по имени Joanna verch Rhys Heiress of STACKPOLE в 1388 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 23 июля 1403 г. в местности шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 41 год.

Se casó con Joanna verch Rhys Heiress of STACKPOLE en 1388, en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 23 de julio de 1403, en Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 41 años.
